text: Weil developed her philosophy of attention while studying under the mathematician Alain at the Lycée Henri-IV in Paris. Alain taught that schoolwork, done rightly, was not about acquiring information but about training the faculty of attention itself — and that this faculty was transferable. A student who truly attends to a Latin translation, Weil absorbed, is developing the same capacity that will later allow them to attend to a suffering human being. The subject matter is almost incidental; what matters is practicing the posture of receptive, patient waiting. Academic difficulty, on this view, is spiritual exercise in disguise.
question: According to Weil's account of Alain's teaching, what is the real purpose of difficult schoolwork?

options.text: Acquiring domain knowledge that transfers to professional life
Developing the transferable faculty of attention itself
Cultivating humility by confronting one's intellectual limits
